generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor coupled with cycloidal gear transmission to drive the robot axis. Controlled via Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller with closed-loop capabilities."
]

instance CycloidalGearTransmission : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Cycloidal gear transmission system interfacing with the permanent magnet synchronous motor to provide precise motion control for the robot axis."
]

instance SiemensSINUMERIK840DController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller managing remote actuation and closed-loop control with hardware/software safety limits for the robot's motion system."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorShaftEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-resolution position encoder mounted directly on the motor shaft to measure input angle with time-series sampling of input current."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorShaftDataTransmitted
]

instance GearboxOutputEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary position encoder positioned behind the gearbox to measure output angle with high-resolution spatial data for motion monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedData GearboxOutputDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorShaftDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorShaftEncoder
    base:desc "Automated one-way transmission of motor shaft position and current data via MQTT protocol for digital shadow aggregation."
]

instance GearboxOutputDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearboxOutputEncoder
    base:desc "Automated one-way transmission of gearbox output angle data via MQTT protocol for digital shadow aggregation."
]

instance ContextualDataTraces : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorShaftEncoder, GearboxOutputEncoder
    base:desc "Aggregated contextual data traces including operational states, sensor readings, and environmental parameters collected for friction identification analysis."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLFunctionalArchitecture : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML-based functional architecture model defining component-connector perspectives using Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs) for the robot system."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLToolchainEnabler
]

instance SysMLStateMachineModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML v1.7 state machine model describing behavioral dynamics of the robot's control system for friction loss detection."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLToolchainEnabler
]

instance MontiArcAutomatonModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MontiArc Automaton model for behavioral modeling of the robot's operational states and control logic."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MontiArcAutomatonEnabler
]

instance SimulinkDynamicSimulation : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink model for detailed dynamic simulation of the robot's kinematic and friction behavior."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance FrictionModelData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured digital representation of friction characteristics derived from contextual data traces for analytical purposes."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ContextualDataTraces
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler, MontiArcAutomatonEnabler
]

instance KinematicStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed kinematic state data including position, velocity, and acceleration derived from sensor readings."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorShaftDataTransmitted, GearboxOutputDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance OperationalStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated operational state data including sensor readings, environmental parameters, and system health metrics."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ContextualDataTraces
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLToolchainEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLToolchainEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML-based MBSE toolchain including Papyrus, Cameo Systems Modeler, or MagicDraw for multi-layered architecture modeling."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAggregationService, VisualizationService
]

instance MontiArcAutomatonEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MontiArc framework for behavioral modeling and state machine implementation in the Digital Twin system."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionAnalysisService
]

instance SimulinkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink environment for dynamic system simulation and control logic implementation."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComparativeAnalysisService
]

instance MQTTDataAggregator : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT-based middleware for real-time data acquisition and one-way transmission from physical sensors to digital shadow."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAggregationService
]

instance CppJavaImplementation : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "C++/Java-based implementation for low-level control logic and high-performance computations in the Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComparativeAnalysisService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataAggregationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated collection and aggregation of contextual data traces from physical system via one-way data flow without active intervention."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataAggregationInsight
]

instance AbstractionContextualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Processing and abstraction of raw sensor data into structured digital representations for specific analytical purposes."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionModelInsight
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Presentation of aggregated data in dashboards or UML-based models (e.g., class diagrams) for stakeholder interpretation."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemBehaviorInsight
]

instance ComparativeAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Comparative analysis of simulated data against measured data to detect friction loss or deviations in physical system behavior."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionDeviationInsight
]

instance FrictionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Specialized service for friction identification and analysis using kinematic state data and friction models."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionIdentificationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance DataAggregationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into aggregated operational states and sensor readings for monitoring purposes without active intervention."
]

instance FrictionModelInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structured digital representation of friction characteristics derived from contextual data traces for analytical purposes."
]

instance SystemBehaviorInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visualized relationships between physical and digital representations through dashboards and UML-based models."
]

instance FrictionDeviationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detection of friction loss or deviations through comparative analysis of simulated vs. measured data."
]

instance FrictionIdentificationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of friction characteristics in the robot's motion system through specialized analysis services."
]

// C1: Environment
instance RobotMotionControlEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Precision motion control environment for the MABI MAX 100 serial robot with six axes."
    base:contains PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor,
                CycloidalGearTransmission,
                SiemensSINUMERIK840DController,
                MotorShaftEncoder,
                GearboxOutputEncoder
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system is orchestrated through a **multi-layered MBSE approach**, incorporating **requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers** as defined by methodologies like **RFLP, OOSEM, SPES, or SMArDT**. **Architecture description languages (ADLs)** such as **ACME, AADL, MontiArc, or SysML IBDs** provide the foundational **component & connector perspective**, enabling **modular integration** of heterogeneous tools (e.g., **MathWorks Simulink, other simulation tools, or general-purpose languages like C++/Java**). While the document does not explicitly detail **system-wide coordination mechanisms** (e.g., event-driven orchestration, hybrid architectures, or resource management), the integration of **multiple modeling notations and tools** implies a **loosely coupled, hybrid architecture** where behavioral descriptions (e.g., state machines in SysML v1.7 or MontiArc Automaton) are combined with simulation and code for dynamic system behavior."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin lifecycle stages, as inferred from the provided context, align with **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies and are structured across **four hierarchical abstraction layers**: **requirements**, **functional architecture**, **logical architecture**, and **product/technical layer**. These stages represent distinct phases in the Digital Twin's development and operation:
  1. **Requirements Phase**: Defines functional and non-functional requirements for the Digital Twin, ensuring alignment with stakeholder needs and system objectives.
  2. **Functional Architecture Phase**: Uses **architecture description languages (ADLs)** (e.g., ACME, AADL, MontiArc, or SysML Internal Block Diagrams) to model the system's **components, interfaces, interconnections, and composition**, abstracting high-level functional behavior without physical constraints.
  3. **Logical Architecture Phase**: Refines the functional model into a **logical architecture**, incorporating **behavioral descriptions** (e.g., MontiArc Automaton, SysML state machines) to define dynamic interactions and state transitions.
  4. **Product/Technical Layer**: Implements the Digital Twin in **simulation tools** (e.g., MathWorks Simulink, other simulation frameworks) or **general-purpose programming languages** (e.g., C++, Java) to achieve executable representations of the physical system.
  Transition management between these phases is governed by **MBSE methodologies** (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM, SPES, SMArDT), which ensure consistency and traceability across abstraction levels. The **Digital Shadow Reference Model** (Fig. 1) further contextualizes this lifecycle by emphasizing **one-way data aggregation** from physical objects to digital representations, implying a **passive-to-active evolution** where the Digital Twin integrates active components (e.g., analytics, decision support) during later stages. Governance is implicit in the **heterogeneous stakeholder integration** requirement, necessitating cross-disciplinary validation at each phase."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system validates model fidelity by comparing **simulated data** (e.g., from MBSE tools like Simulink) against **measured data** (e.g., encoder time-series, current inputs). **Uncertainty sources** arise from **data abstraction** and **aggregation processes** in the digital shadow pipeline, though no explicit **quantification methods** or **error correction mechanisms** are described. **Quality assurance** relies on **contextual data trace consistency**, but structured **validation and verification (V&V) processes** (e.g., formal proofs, statistical testing) or **error detection thresholds** are not mentioned in the provided documents."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Shadow system leverages **MQTT** as the communication protocol for transmitting sensor time series data from the physical object to its digital representation. While the document confirms the use of MQTT, it does not provide details on **network topology, bandwidth/latency requirements, reliability/redundancy mechanisms, security protocols, network segmentation, QoS policies, or edge-gateway configurations**. The protocol is described as lightweight and suitable for IoT/real-time applications, but no further technical specifications for the underlying network infrastructure are mentioned."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates with external systems through **standardized modeling languages and tools** such as **SysML, ACME, AADL, MontiArc, and MathWorks Simulink**, which facilitate **interoperability** between heterogeneous components. However, the document does not specify **concrete data exchange protocols** (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT, REST APIs) or **enterprise system dependencies** (e.g., Manufacturing Execution Systems, ERP, or SCADA). The focus is on **internal system modeling** rather than external system integration, though the use of **ADLs and simulation tools** suggests potential for **plug-and-play integration** via standardized interfaces. No explicit mention is made of **Digital Twin-to-Digital Twin communication** or **enterprise-wide coordination frameworks**."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to **standardized modeling and architecture frameworks** to ensure **interoperability, consistency, and compliance** across development phases. Key standards and specifications include:
  - **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) Standards**: The system leverages **SysML (Systems Modeling Language)** as the primary **architecture description language (ADL)** for modeling components, connectors, and behavior (e.g., state machines, automata). SysML’s **Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)** and **requirements modeling** provide a **formalized structure** for Digital Twin development.
  - **Architecture Description Languages (ADLs)**: Additional ADLs such as **ACME**, **AADL**, and **MontiArc** are used to define **component-based architectures** and **interconnection logic**, ensuring modularity and reusability.
  - **Simulation and Implementation Standards**: The Digital Twin integrates with **MathWorks Simulink** for **dynamic system simulation**, adhering to its **modeling and execution standards**. General-purpose languages like **C++ and Java** are used for implementation, with **no explicit standardization constraints** mentioned beyond tool-specific best practices.
  - **Digital Shadow Reference Model Compliance**: The system aligns with the **Digital Shadow Reference Model** (as defined by Kritzinger et al.), which standardizes **one-way data flows** from physical objects to digital representations. This model enforces **contextual data aggregation** and **abstraction** as foundational principles, ensuring traceability and purpose-driven digital representations.
  - **Interoperability**: While the document does not specify **data interchange standards** (e.g., OPC UA, MTConnect), the use of **MBSE and ADLs** inherently supports **cross-tool interoperability** by defining **formal interfaces** and **composition rules** for system components. The **MQTT protocol** is implied for **real-time data transmission** (as referenced in the Digital Shadow context), though not explicitly detailed in the provided text."
]
